title: daucus pusillus var. microphyllus
intro: Daucus pusillus var. microphyllus, commonly known as small carrot or wild carrot, is a perennial plant in the carrot family that is native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Daucus pusillus var. microphyllus include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and provide antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
